reasoning: The question asks if the number of 'Explosions/Remote violence' events in Burkina Faso in the 30 days prior to two specific dates will exceed the 360-day trailing 30-day average (currently ~26.333). Recent ACLED reports throughout 2024 and 2025 highlight a structural shift toward remote violence by both the Burkinabe military (using TB2 drones) and insurgent groups like JNIM (using FPV drone-dropped IEDs). This long-term escalation means recent months naturally exceed older 12-month averages. We are also deeply in the dry season (Nov-May), which typically sees an operational tempo well above the yearly average (pulled down by the wet season). Furthermore, on December 24, 2025, Burkinabe President Traoré announced new 'large-scale joint operations,' perfectly overlapping with our target 30-day windows and highly likely to result in a surge of drone airstrikes and retaliatory IEDs. For the proximate date (Jan 25), the probability is very high (82%) as the major offensive was underway. For the later date (Feb 17), the probability retreats slightly towards the base rate (73%) to reflect greater uncertainty around whether the offensive maintains its intensity.
